mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% on 2018-11-01 reflects recent rate increases
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% on 2018-11-01 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate the negative return signal.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1228k units on 2018-10-01 shows housing market softness
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month moving average signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.39 on 2018-11-01 reflects strong industrial activity
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k jobs per month, signaling a weakening labor market that precedes broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns. This always-on trend mechanism operates without requiring an explicit trigger.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149893k on 2018-11-01 shows strong employment levels
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where PAYEMS growth is below 100k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) growth accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, the Fed is in restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% and CPIAUCSL at 252.88 on 2018-11-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and inflation accelerates, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling insufficient aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings and equity valuations. The mechanism is regime-specific to late-cycle expansions where growth deceleration is most predictive of equity weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18671.65 billion on 2018-07-01 reflects solid economic activity
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above their 6-month moving average while retail sales (RSAFS) remain stable or growing, signaling healthy consumer demand and housing investment that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 6-month MA AND RSAFS month-over-month change >= 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1228k and RSAFS at 513531M on 2018-10-01 and 2018-11-01
regime_hint: expansion with robust housing and consumption demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 45% of instances where HOUST exceeds 6-month MA and RSAFS is stable, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
